name: Clermont-Dessous Ridges Loop
name_original: Clermont-Dessous – boucle de course à pied
description: This moderate 3.5-mile (5.7 km) jogging loop in Clermont-Dessous offers scenic views of the Garonne valley and historic village sights.
region_intro: Jogging routes Clermont-Dessous offer exploration of this elevated region in Lot-et-Garonne, France. The area is characterized by panoramic views of the Garonne valley and surrounding Gascony hills. Running routes often traverse picturesque orchards and vineyards, providing varied terrain. The village itself is a fortified settlement with a medieval character, adding historical interest to outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Clermont-Dessous The most popular jogging route is Lusignan-Grand Panoramic View Loop, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views across the Garonne valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Clermont-Dessous Ridges Loop, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.7 km) path. This route explores the elevated ridges around the village, providing varied landscapes. Local runners also love the Running loop from Arnauchon, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through the rural landscape, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Clermont-Dessous is defined by Garonne valley views, agricultural lands with orchards and vineyards, and the historic village itself. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Clermont-Dessous are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Clermont-Dessous's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Clermont-Dessous?
A: There are over 100 running routes around Clermont-Dessous, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging trails, with 10 easy routes, 82 moderate routes, and 16 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails near Clermont-Dessous?
A: The jogging trails around Clermont-Dessous offer a varied and scenic experience. You can expect paths through picturesque orchards and vineyards, as well as grassy or earth surfaces. While generally accessible, some sections may include uneven ground and small hills, providing a good mix of challenges and smooth running.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Clermont-Dessous?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, many of the easier and moderate routes through orchards and vineyards are suitable for families. The generally accessible terrain and scenic views make for enjoyable outings. Consider routes with less elevation gain for younger runners.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Clermont-Dessous?
A: Yes, many of the outdoor trails in the Clermont-Dessous area are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy your run with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control, and be mindful of local regulations, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock.

Q: What are the best times of year to go jogging in Clermont-Dessous?
A: Clermont-Dessous offers beautiful running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes as orchards bloom or leaves change color. Summer mornings and evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter runs can be refreshing, offering crisp air and unique views of the Garonne valley.

Q: Are there running routes that offer panoramic views of the Garonne valley?
A: Absolutely! Clermont-Dessous is renowned for its breathtaking panoramic views. Many routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001306405/lusignan-grand-panoramic-view-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lusignan-Grand Panoramic View Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to showcase sweeping vistas of the Garonne and Masse valleys, and even the distant Pyrenean peaks on clear days. The village's elevated position within the 'Coteaux du Pays de Serres' guarantees stunning scenery.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while running in Clermont-Dessous?
A: Clermont-Dessous itself is a significant attraction, being a picturesque, fortified village with a medieval character. Running through the village allows you to explore old houses, a 12th-century church, and the remnants of its castle. Additionally, nearby attractions you might encounter or wish to visit include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/598317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prayssas Village Square&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Christopher's Church in Vianne&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Clermont-Dessous are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001292029/clermont-dessous-ridges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clermont-Dessous Ridges Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that explores the elevated ridges around the village.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Clermont-Dessous?
A: Parking is generally available within Clermont-Dessous village itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for many routes. For specific trailheads outside the village, local parking areas are often indicated. It's advisable to check individual route descriptions for precise parking information.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Clermont-Dessous?
A: The running experience in Clermont-Dessous is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning Garonne valley views, the peaceful agricultural landscapes with orchards and vineyards, and the charm of the historic village itself. The variety of terrain and well-maintained paths also receive positive feedback.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24588754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Prayssas – Prayssas Village Square loop from Prayssas&lt;/a&gt; offer distances of over 14 km. These routes provide more extensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes and can include greater elevation changes, suitable for experienced runners.

Q: Can I combine my run with a visit to a cafe or restaurant?
A: Clermont-Dessous, as a charming village, offers local amenities where you might find a cafe or restaurant to refuel after your run. Exploring the village center after your activity is a great way to experience local hospitality and enjoy the historic surroundings. For more options, nearby towns also provide a wider selection.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Clermont-Dessous running loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this running loop is approximately 40 minutes, based on an average jogging pace. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, generally considered a moderate run. You'll encounter mostly accessible paths, but there are sections with uneven ground and small hills. Expect paths through orchards, vineyards, and grassy or earth surfaces, requiring some sure-footedness.

faqs.question: Is the Clermont-Dessous running loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, the loop requires good fitness due to its varied terrain and elevation changes. It's not ideal for absolute beginners, but those with some running experience and sure-footedness should find it manageable. There are no overly steep or technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking panoramic views of the Garonne valley and the surrounding Gascony hills. The route passes through picturesque rural landscapes, including orchards and vineyards. On clear days, you might even catch a glimpse of the Pyrenean peaks. The historic village of Clermont-Dessous itself, perched on a rocky outcrop, adds to the scenic beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers several points of interest. You'll pass through the historic village of Clermont-Dessous, which features majestic architecture, including a 12th-century fortified church and remnants of an old château. The Saint John the Baptist Church is a specific highlight along the way. Additionally, there are numerous spots offering panoramic views over the Garonne plains.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Clermont-Dessous running loop?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details are not provided in the available information. However, for trails starting in villages like Clermont-Dessous, it's common to find public parking areas near the village center or close to local amenities. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Clermont-Dessous running loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The available information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or specific leash rules. Generally, trails in rural areas of France are dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to access the Clermont-Dessous running loop. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes (blossoming orchards or autumn colors). Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon run is advisable. Winter conditions might include muddy paths, but generally, the region experiences mild winters.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a rural loop, the village of Clermont-Dessous, where the loop is centered, may offer local establishments for refreshments. It's advisable to check local listings or inquire upon arrival for cafes or restaurants in the immediate vicinity.

faqs.question: Does this running loop intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the official trail named Impasse de Baladié, covering approximately 22% of its length. This might offer opportunities to extend or connect to other local paths if you wish.
